title: papuacedrus papuana
intro: Papuacedrus papuana, also known as the Kawakawa tree, is a tropical evergreen tree native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in Papua New Guinea. The plant has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties. The health benefits of Papuacedrus papuana include relief from fever, pain, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural insect repellent. The herbal preparations of Papuacedrus papuana include tea infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and health.
